title: Involving older pupils in free breakfast clubs
body: "Background  Silver Springs Primary Academy in Stalybridge now offers a free breakfast club available to all pupils through the Department for Education’s free breakfast club early adopters scheme.   Early adopter schools are funded to deliver a 30 minute breakfast club, free and available to all pupils, which includes food. Schools have the flexibility to deliver the free breakfast club in a way that best meets the needs of their school, pupils and families.  How they staff the breakfast club  The free breakfast club is delivered by:   teaching assistants breakfast club staff volunteer ‘breakfast club baristas’ from years 5 and 6 staff from special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) classes   Pupils can apply to be involved as breakfast club baristas to help the younger children.  How SEND is supported  The school guarantees the needs of pupils with SEND are met by ensuring that the staff from each of their SEND classes are involved in providing the breakfast club. This means that pupils are always supported by a familiar face who knows them and their needs well.  The food offering  Each day the pupils get a variety of food to choose from including:   fresh fruit platters cereals yogurt toast crumpets bagels breakfast muffins malt loaf cheese beans   Where the club takes place  The school serves the breakfast in classrooms and once pupils have finished eating they start early independent work which includes:   reading spelling practice maths retrieval practice mindful colouring education apps   Feedback and impact  The school has observed that:   pupils are responding well to the free club, especially the breakfast club baristas, and the numbers attending are steadily increasing it has improved attendance and punctuality it is encouraging healthy eating habits the club offers a good opportunity for children to socialise with their peers promoting confidence and interpersonal skills   Parents report that:   it helps with the morning routine, with a smoother transition into school which reduces their stress and anxiety it supports their child’s emotional wellbeing and social development   "

taxons.title: School food, accommodation, transport and uniform
